steps.title: Set the equations equal and solve for x
steps.content:
Since both expressions equal $y$, the solution point must satisfy both equations. Set them equal:
$$5x + 4 = 5x^2 + 4$$

Simplify:
  • Subtract 4: $5x = 5x^2$
  • Rearrange: $5x^2 - 5x = 0$
  • Factor: $5x(x - 1) = 0$
  • Solutions: $x = 0$ or $x = 1$

steps.title: Evaluate y and match to the choices
steps.content:
• When $x = 0$: $y = 5(0) + 4 = 4$ → $(0, 4)$
  • When $x = 1$: $y = 5(1) + 4 = 9$ → $(1, 9)$
The choice $(0, 4)$ appears in the options. That matches choice B — B is correct.
